# Git Commit Message Conventions

Please create commit messages that adhere to the following rules:

## 1. Format Structure

- Every commit must start with a suitable emoji from the list below.
- Format: <emoji> <title>
- If the content exceeds 70 characters, the details must be moved to the description section (separated from the title by a blank line).

## 2. Emoji List (Gitmoji)

- ✨ :sparkles: - New Feature
- 🐛 :bug: - Bug Fix
- 📝 :memo: - Documentation update
- ♻️ :recycle: - Code refactoring
- ⚡ :zap: - Performance improvement
- 🔥 :fire: - Removing code or unnecessary files
- 🎨 :art: - Improving code structure/formatting (Style)
- ✅ :white_check_mark: - Adding/Updating tests
- 🔧 :wrench: - Configuration changes
- 📦 :package: - Updating dependencies/libraries
- 🚀 :rocket: - Deployment/Deploy
- 💄 :lipstick: - Updating UI/UX & CSS
- 🔒 :lock: - Security
- 🚨 :rotating_light: - Fixing compiler/linter warnings
- 🚧 :construction: - Work in progress (WIP)

## 3. Content Guidelines

- **Language:** Always use 100% English for both Title and Description.
- **Format:** <emoji> <title>
- **Title Rules:**
  - Concise, not exceeding 70 characters.
  - Use imperative action verbs (Add, Fix, Remove, Optimize, Update).
- **Description Rules:**
  - For complex changes, provide a detailed explanation using bullet points in English.
